title: Doc-truth audit method (re-verified after dark-first site flip)
summary: Cross check every documented kage command/tool against reality: extract 'kage <sub ' and 'kage <tool ' mentions from docs/ .html + README, diff against command === dispatch strings in mcp/cli.ts and name: "kage " registr
body: Cross-check every documented kage command/tool against reality: extract 'kage <sub>' and 'kage_<tool>' mentions from docs/*.html + README, diff against command === dispatch strings in mcp/cli.ts and name: "kage_*" registry in mcp/index.ts. Found+fixed 3 doc lies on 2026-06-12 (phantom kage_org_*/kage_layered_recall/kage_global_build, and 'kage propose-from-diff' → 'kage propose --from-diff'). Reverse diff (real-but-undocumented) is informational — the guide's MCP section is intentionally curated and says so. Still valid after the dark-first theme flip; theme changes don't affect command truth. Rerun after any CLI/MCP surface change.
